title: India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership 2026-2030: Semiconductors, Green Energy, Critical Minerals
lede: India and the Netherlands formalized a five-year strategic roadmap during PM's May 2026 visit, anchoring cooperation across semiconductors, renewable energy, critical minerals, and agricultural technology with 14 bilateral outcomes.

sections.context: The Prime Minister's three-day visit to the Netherlands (May 15-17, 2026) produced the overarching Roadmap of India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership [2026-2030], signaling a deliberate upgrade in bilateral engagement across multiple sectors. The timing reflects mutual interest in deepening ties on energy transition and technology. The roadmap encompasses fourteen distinct bilateral documents spanning semiconductors, critical minerals, water management, green hydrogen, and agricultural-dairy cooperation. Notable is the TATA Electronics-ASML MoU to support semiconductor fabrication in Dholera, positioning Netherlands' advanced semiconductor ecosystem expertise to anchor India's domestic fab capacity. The strategic partnership comes as both nations navigate global supply chain diversification and energy security imperatives.
sections.threads: India-Netherlands cooperation on critical minerals aligns with broader New Delhi initiatives to secure supply chains independent of concentrated suppliers, echoing similar bilateral mineral partnerships India has pursued across Africa and Southeast Asia. The green hydrogen roadmap sits within India's larger renewable energy transition framework and complements domestic National Green Hydrogen Mission targets. Agricultural cooperation through Centers of Excellence in flowers (West Tripura) and dairy (Bengaluru) reflects India's agricultural modernization strategy and Dutch expertise in high-value, technology-intensive farming. The customs mutual administrative assistance agreement extends India's ongoing work to strengthen trade and tax compliance infrastructure with developed economies, part of a wider digitalization push across bilateral relationships.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor implementation of the Dholera semiconductor fab partnership between TATA and ASML—this represents a critical test of whether India can attract and retain advanced semiconductor manufacturing. The Joint Working Group on Renewable Energy and NITI Aayog collaboration on energy transition should be tracked for policy harmonization and project pipeline development over the next 12-18 months. Progress on the Kalpasar Project water cooperation letter of intent warrants attention as a potential model for Dutch-Indian technical transfer on water management. Restitution of Chola Copper Plates signals cultural diplomacy gains; monitor whether this precedent accelerates return of Indian artifacts from European holdings and shapes bilateral heritage cooperation.
